description: Manage the always-on Cody Slack cc-connect bridge

list.usage_instructions: List all Cody bridge health checks. Use --table/-t for readable output, --limit/-l to cap rows, --filter/-f with field:op:value such as ok:eq:true, and --properties/-p to select fields.

get.help: Get a single Cody bridge health check by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get one health check by required CHECK_ID, such as service-running or old-user-deactivated. Use --table/-t for a single-row table.

checks.usage_instructions: Run health checks across the LaunchAgent, config files, Keychain token presence, and Slack identity state. Use checks list for all results and checks get CHECK_ID for one check.

show.help: Show configured Cody bridge paths and identifiers.
show.usage_instructions: Show configured paths and IDs for the Cody bridge. Use --table/-t for a compact summary of app ID, bot user, DM channel, and config path.

config.usage_instructions: Show static Cody bridge paths and Slack identifiers. Use this before editing LaunchAgent, wrapper, or cc-connect config files.

status.usage_instructions: Show LaunchAgent load and runtime state. Use --table/-t for a compact label, loaded, running, PID, and state view.

start.usage_instructions: Bootstrap the Cody cc-connect LaunchAgent from its plist. Use when the service is not loaded; use service restart for an already loaded service.

stop.help: Boot out the Cody cc-connect LaunchAgent.
stop.usage_instructions: Boot out the Cody cc-connect LaunchAgent. This stops the always-on Slack bridge until service start is run again.

restart.usage_instructions: Restart the loaded Cody cc-connect LaunchAgent with launchctl kickstart -k. Use after changing config, wrapper, or token state.

logs.usage_instructions: Show recent Cody bridge logs. Use --stream/-s stdout or stderr and --lines/-l to choose line count; default stream is stdout and default lines is 80.

service.usage_instructions: Manage the macOS LaunchAgent for the Cody cc-connect bridge. Use status before start, stop, or restart so the current service state is explicit.

app.help: Verify and test the Cody Slack app

verify.help: Verify the new Cody bot and old Cody member state.
verify.usage_instructions: Verify the active Cody bot and old Cody user through the Slack CLI default profile. Use --table/-t to confirm the new bot is not deleted and the old user is deleted.

send-test.help: Send a test message from the Cody app into Adam's Cody DM.

arguments.name: text
arguments.help: Message text to send to Adam's Cody app DM

send-test.usage_instructions: Send a required TEXT argument from the Cody Slack app into Adam Cody app DM. The command uses the bot token from Keychain and does not print the token.

app.usage_instructions: Verify and test the Cody Slack app identity. Use app verify to confirm the new bot and old user state, and app send-test to send a bot-authored smoke message to Adam Cody DM.

status.help: Show whether required Slack tokens exist in Keychain.
status.usage_instructions: Show whether CODY_SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and CODY_SLACK_APP_TOKEN exist in Keychain. Use --table/-t for service, account, and present columns.

tokens.usage_instructions: Check required Keychain token presence for the Slack app bridge. This group reports whether token services exist and never prints token values.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth login` to configure local bridge authentication.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth logout` to clear stored credentials.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth status` to verify local bridge authentication.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth test` to run live bridge authentication checks.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles list` to list saved profiles.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles get` to inspect one profile.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles create` to create a new profile.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles set-default` to select the default profile.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles delete` to remove a profile.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth profiles` to manage named profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `cc-connect-slack-manager auth` to manage bridge authentication and profiles.

usage_instructions: Use cc-connect-slack-manager to inspect and manage the always-on Cody Slack app bridge in ATA Learning. Commands follow cc-connect-slack-manager <group> <action>; JSON is the default and --table/-t is available on status-style commands. Never use this tool to reveal Slack token values; token commands only report presence.
